description.source: You can build survey using our in-house developed survey tool
description.target: You can build survey using our in-house developed survey tool

body:0001.source: This article is machine translated from english
body:0001.target: This article is machine translated from english

body:0002.source: What is the in-platform survey tool?
body:0002.target: What is the in-platform survey tool?

body:0003.source: The GoVocal in-platform survey tool lets admins and project managers create and manage surveys entirely within the platform, no external tools required. It supports:
body:0003.target: The GoVocal in-platform survey tool lets admins and project managers create and manage surveys entirely within the platform, no external tools required. It supports:

body:0004.source: A wide variety of question types (short answer, multiple choice, linear scale, mapping questions, etc.)
body:0004.target: A wide variety of question types (short answer, multiple choice, linear scale, mapping questions, etc.)

body:0005.source: Real-time result tracking and Excel exports
body:0005.target: Real-time result tracking and Excel exports

body:0006.source: Optional logic to personalize survey flows
body:0006.target: Optional logic to personalize survey flows

body:0007.source: Full integration with platform access rights and anonymity settings
body:0007.target: Full integration with platform access rights and anonymity settings

body:0008.source: It’s designed to lower barriers for participation, consolidate data collection, and streamline reporting.
body:0008.target: It’s designed to lower barriers for participation, consolidate data collection, and streamline reporting.

body:0009.source: What are the key capabilities of the survey tool?
body:0009.target: What are the key capabilities of the survey tool?

body:0010.source: Different Question Types: from short/long text to advanced mapping and ESRI shapefile upload
body:0010.target: Different Question Types: from short/long text to advanced mapping and ESRI shapefile upload

body:0011.source: Pages: organize long surveys into sections; include normal pages or map-based pages with contextual layers
body:0011.target: Pages: organize long surveys into sections; include normal pages or map-based pages with contextual layers

body:0012.source: Logic: route users to relevant questions or pages based on answers
body:0012.target: Logic: route users to relevant questions or pages based on answers

body:0013.source: Anonymity toggle: allow anonymous submissions (see: Enabling anonymous participation)
body:0013.target: Anonymity toggle: allow anonymous submissions (see: Enabling anonymous participation)

body:0014.source: Reporting: generate project reports, download raw data, and visualize mapping questions
body:0014.target: Reporting: generate project reports, download raw data, and visualize mapping questions

body:0015.source: Limitations: some question types (e.g. mapping, file upload) can’t be bulk-imported (see: Bulk import paper ideas)
body:0015.target: Limitations: some question types (e.g. mapping, file upload) can’t be bulk-imported (see: Bulk import paper ideas)

body:0016.source: Saved answers if you exit early: if you wish to exist the survey and come back later your answers will be saved so you don't have to start again from scratch! and email will also be sent to you to remind you to finish and submit the survey you started.
body:0016.target: Saved answers if you exit early: if you wish to exist the survey and come back later your answers will be saved so you don't have to start again from scratch! and email will also be sent to you to remind you to finish and submit the survey you started.

body:0017.source: What are the different question types?
body:0017.target: What are the different question types?

body:0020.source: in the settings of each question type if you scroll to the bottom there is a toggle that you can turn on called Make answering this question required - you can toggle it on to make the question required or leave it off to make the question optional
body:0020.target: in the settings of each question type if you scroll to the bottom there is a toggle that you can turn on called Make answering this question required - you can toggle it on to make the question required or leave it off to make the question optional

body:0021.source: Given a title, description, and extra instructions
body:0021.target: Given a title, description, and extra instructions

body:0023.source: Short answer / Long answer (with AI summarization if enabled)
body:0023.target: Short answer / Long answer (with AI summarization if enabled)

body:0024.source: Single choice (radio buttons; supports “Other,” randomize order, dropdown view)
body:0024.target: Single choice (radio buttons; supports “Other,” randomize order, dropdown view)

body:0026.source: Image choice (choose between images; can limit required selections)
body:0026.target: Image choice (choose between images; can limit required selections)

body:0029.source: Ranking (drag‑and‑drop or number input for priority order)
body:0029.target: Ranking (drag‑and‑drop or number input for priority order)

body:0033.source: File upload (up to 50MB; files stored as URLs in exports)
body:0033.target: File upload (up to 50MB; files stored as URLs in exports)

body:0034.source: Only 1 file can be uploaded per question. To allow for more files, create a new file upload question
body:0034.target: Only 1 file can be uploaded per question. To allow for more files, create a new file upload question

body:0035.source: ⚠️ Ranking, Matrix, File Upload, and all Mapping questions cannot be uploaded back via offline imports.
body:0035.target: ⚠️ Ranking, Matrix, File Upload, and all Mapping questions cannot be uploaded back via offline imports.

body:0042.source:
Each mapping question collects one input per map.
→ To gather multiple inputs on the same map, create multiple mapping questions.
body:0042.target:
Each mapping question collects one input per map.
→ To gather multiple inputs on the same map, create multiple mapping questions.

body:0043.source: Every mapping question and map layout can be configured individually to highlight different areas.
body:0043.target: Every mapping question and map layout can be configured individually to highlight different areas.

body:0044.source: To customize your map, click “Configure map”, you can:
body:0044.target: To customize your map, click “Configure map”, you can:

body:0046.source: Add GeoJSON layers, or ESRI maps if your account has access to this feature
body:0046.target: Add GeoJSON layers, or ESRI maps if your account has access to this feature

body:0047.source: ⚠️ When using an ESRI-map, you can not use the on-click functionality on ESRI's side as this conflicts with our platform's on-click behaviour.
body:0047.target: ⚠️ When using an ESRI-map, you can not use the on-click functionality on ESRI's side as this conflicts with our platform's on-click behaviour.

body:0048.source: How to create or duplicate a survey?
body:0048.target: How to create or duplicate a survey?

body:0051.source: In the project creation flow, select Create a survey as the engagement method
body:0051.target: In the project creation flow, select Create a survey as the engagement method

body:0052.source: Or, to add a survey to an existing project, go to the project's Phases tab, click Add phase, and select Survey as the engagement method
body:0052.target: Or, to add a survey to an existing project, go to the project's Phases tab, click Add phase, and select Survey as the engagement method

body:0054.source: Navigate to the Survey Form tab
body:0054.target: Navigate to the Survey Form tab

body:0055.source: Click Edit survey form to open the builder
body:0055.target: Click Edit survey form to open the builder

body:0056.source: Add pages (normal or map-based) and drag in questions
body:0056.target: Add pages (normal or map-based) and drag in questions

body:0058.source: Add titles, descriptions, videos, or images to pages
body:0058.target: Add titles, descriptions, videos, or images to pages

body:0061.source: Toggle on Demographic questions asked to users in phase settings > Phase access and user data to capture demographic data at the end of the survey
body:0061.target: Toggle on Demographic questions asked to users in phase settings > Phase access and user data to capture demographic data at the end of the survey

body:0063.source: Use the View Survey button to preview.
body:0063.target: Use the View Survey button to preview.

body:0064.source: Submit test responses, then delete test data before launch.
body:0064.target: Submit test responses, then delete test data before launch.

body:0066.source: Go to the Insights tab of your survey phase, then turn on the Open for answers toggle at the top of the page.
body:0066.target: Go to the Insights tab of your survey phase, then turn on the Open for answers toggle at the top of the page.

body:0068.source: Configure a new project/phase as a survey
body:0068.target: Configure a new project/phase as a survey

body:0070.source: Click Duplicate another survey and choose from your existing surveys
body:0070.target: Click Duplicate another survey and choose from your existing surveys

body:0071.source: How to apply logic to a survey?
body:0071.target: How to apply logic to a survey?

body:0072.source: Question‑level logic: Route users to different pages based on answers (single‑select or linear scale only).
body:0072.target: Question‑level logic: Route users to different pages based on answers (single‑select or linear scale only).

body:0073.source: Page‑level logic: Decide what the next page is, regardless of responses.
body:0073.target: Page‑level logic: Decide what the next page is, regardless of responses.

body:0075.source: “If any other question” logic (one option routes differently; all others go elsewhere)
body:0075.target: “If any other question” logic (one option routes differently; all others go elsewhere)

body:0078.source: Our survey logic helps you control which page respondents see next based on their answers. However, there are some important limitations to keep in mind:
body:0078.target: Our survey logic helps you control which page respondents see next based on their answers. However, there are some important limitations to keep in mind:

body:0079.source:
Logic only triggers when clicking “Next”
Logic is evaluated when respondents move to the next page, not instantly after answering a question.
body:0079.target:
Logic only triggers when clicking “Next”
Logic is evaluated when respondents move to the next page, not instantly after answering a question.

body:0080.source:
No backward logic
You can’t send respondents back to a previous page using logic.
body:0080.target:
No backward logic
You can’t send respondents back to a previous page using logic.

body:0081.source:
No intra-page logic
Logic always applies to pages, not individual questions.
→ If you want logic to jump to a specific question, place that question on its own page.
body:0081.target:
No intra-page logic
Logic always applies to pages, not individual questions.
→ If you want logic to jump to a specific question, place that question on its own page.

body:0082.source:
Supported question types
Logic can only be applied to single-select and linear scale questions.
Multi-select, ranking, matrix, number, and mapping questions do not support logic.
body:0082.target:
Supported question types
Logic can only be applied to single-select and linear scale questions.
Multi-select, ranking, matrix, number, and mapping questions do not support logic.

body:0083.source:
Required questions
Any single-select or linear scale question with logic is automatically marked as required.
body:0083.target:
Required questions
Any single-select or linear scale question with logic is automatically marked as required.

body:0085.source: See all answers in real‑time under Insights tab.
body:0085.target: See all answers in real‑time under Insights tab.

body:0086.source: Export data as Excel by pressing on the dots next to the Open for responses toggle (includes URLs to images/files)
body:0086.target: Export data as Excel by pressing on the dots next to the Open for responses toggle (includes URLs to images/files)

body:0087.source: To see individual survey responses: you have to download the survey results. This will give you an excel sheet with individual responses listed in a row
body:0087.target: To see individual survey responses: you have to download the survey results. This will give you an excel sheet with individual responses listed in a row

body:0089.source: To delete specific survey submissions, contact support and provide the submission ID of the submissions you want to delete.
body:0089.target: To delete specific survey submissions, contact support and provide the submission ID of the submissions you want to delete.

body:0090.source: The submission ID can be found when you export the survey results to .xslx. The first column, title ID, is the submission ID.
body:0090.target: The submission ID can be found when you export the survey results to .xslx. The first column, title ID, is the submission ID.

body:0092.source: Use the Project Report feature to create sharable reports (PDF or online)
body:0092.target: Use the Project Report feature to create sharable reports (PDF or online)

body:0096.source: Mapping: Aggregated map view (Drop Pins support heatmap display)
body:0096.target: Mapping: Aggregated map view (Drop Pins support heatmap display)

body:0097.source: For advanced mapping analysis, export GeoJSON/ESRI data and use tools like Mapshaper.
body:0097.target: For advanced mapping analysis, export GeoJSON/ESRI data and use tools like Mapshaper.

body:0098.source: For further exploring these features, see our support articles:
body:0098.target: For further exploring these features, see our support articles:

body:0099.source: Creating Reports with Report Builder
body:0099.target: Creating Reports with Report Builder

body:0103.source: No draft phases: Once added, a phase is visible. However, you can toggle Open for responses off, even when the phase is active
body:0103.target: No draft phases: Once added, a phase is visible. However, you can toggle Open for responses off, even when the phase is active

body:0104.source: Offline upload limitations: Ranking, Matrix, File Upload, and Mapping questions cannot be imported back into the platform
body:0104.target: Offline upload limitations: Ranking, Matrix, File Upload, and Mapping questions cannot be imported back into the platform

body:0105.source: Editing surveys live: You can edit at any time, but deleting questions deletes all existing responses
body:0105.target: Editing surveys live: You can edit at any time, but deleting questions deletes all existing responses

body:0108.source: Surveys can include mapping questions, but these are more structured (one pin/line/area per question)
body:0108.target: Surveys can include mapping questions, but these are more structured (one pin/line/area per question)

body:0109.source: Ideation Map phases allow multiple pins and open discussion
body:0109.target: Ideation Map phases allow multiple pins and open discussion

body:0110.source: Use surveys for structured data collection; use map consultations for open, community-driven inputs
body:0110.target: Use surveys for structured data collection; use map consultations for open, community-driven inputs

body:0112.source: Surveys can allow anonymous answers; users get a unique submission ID to request deletion if needed
body:0112.target: Surveys can allow anonymous answers; users get a unique submission ID to request deletion if needed

body:0114.source: If surveys have Authentication set to None (no login/email), we can’t block duplicate submissions because we don’t know who submitted them, and we don’t collect IP addresses. Storing IP addresses would violate GDPR, so we can't do that. To prevent duplicate submissions, Authentication must be set to either account creation or email verification.
body:0114.target: If surveys have Authentication set to None (no login/email), we can’t block duplicate submissions because we don’t know who submitted them, and we don’t collect IP addresses. Storing IP addresses would violate GDPR, so we can't do that. To prevent duplicate submissions, Authentication must be set to either account creation or email verification.

body:0115.source: Requiring email or account creation enforces one submission per user
body:0115.target: Requiring email or account creation enforces one submission per user
